questions.question: Select the set in which the numbers are related in the same way as are the numbers of the following sets. [31, 49, 712] [42, 64, 633] [NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g. 13 – Operations on 13 such as adding /subtracting /multiplying etc. to 13can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1and 3 is NOT allowed]
questions.explanation: 31×√49=31×7=217 Now, reverse the order of digits in the number 217 = 712 42×√64=42×8=336 = 633 Similarly, 25×√81=25×9=225 = 522

questions.question: What will come in place of the question mark ‘?’if ‘+’ means ‘÷’, ‘÷’ means ‘×’, ‘×’ means ‘-’ and ‘-’ means ‘+’? [729÷9 + 81×4 – 5] − 18= ?
In a certain code language, ‘CLOUD’ is coded as FXDPH and ‘BASKET’ is coded as DBLVJN. How will ‘ENERGY’ be coded in the same language?
questions.explanation: Logic: Letter + place value of letter C+3=F L+12=X O+15=D U+21=P D+4=H Similarly, E+5=J N+14=B E+5=J R+18=J G+7=N Y+25=X Option A Alternate method:- Double the place value CLOUD --> 6,24,30,42,8= FXDPH similarly ENERGY ---> JBJJNX

questions.question: ‘P # Q’ means ‘P is the brother of Q’ ‘P @ Q’ means ‘P is the son of Q’ ‘P & Q’ means ‘P is the wife of Q’ ‘P % Q’ means ‘P is the mother of Q’ In the expression : A @ C & E # G & I @ K, how is C related to G?
questions.explanation: C is Brother's Wife of G. Option B

questions.explanation: Option B

Only conclusion I and II follows
Only conclusion II and III follows

questions.explanation: Clarify antonym → Obscure Sedate antonym → Quick option B

questions.question: The following Venn diagram shows the number of students practising three instruments: Violin, Guitar and Drum. Study the Venn diagram carefully and answer the question that follows: What is the sum of number of students practising only Violin and only Drum?
questions.explanation: From Venn diagram- Only violin = 52 Only Drum = 36 Sum = 52 + 36 = 88

questions.question: Two different positions of the same dice are given below. Which is the number on the face opposite to the face containing 2?
questions.explanation: 6 is present on both the dices along with other 4 numbers. Hence, only 2 is left which must be on the opposite side of 6.

questions.question: Name the sport with which “Ezra Cup” is associated?
questions.explanation: The Ezra Cup is a polo tournament held annually in India by the Calcutta Polo Club.

questions.question: Which was the precursor organisation of the Indian National Congress?
questions.explanation: The Indian Association, founded in 1876 by Surendranath Banerjee and Ananda Mohan Bose, was the first recognised nationalist organisation in British India and a precursor to the Indian National Congress. In contrast, the Indian League was a UK-based organization that aimed to campaign for full independence and self-government for India.

questions.question: The final approval to the five year plans of India is given by?
questions.explanation: The National Development Council [NDC] gave the final approval to the Five-Year Plans in India. The Planning Commission [now replaced by NITI Aayog] used to draft the plans, but they were finalized only after NDC's approval.

questions.question: A cell membrane is considered to be a bilayer [two-layered] because it is made up of:
options: Two layers of protein on either side of a layer of water
Two layers of cholesterol, a lipid
questions.explanation: The cell membrane is composed of a bilayer of phospholipids. Phospholipids have a hydrophilic [water-loving] head and a hydrophobic [water-fearing] tail. In the cell membrane, the hydrophilic heads face the watery environment both inside and outside the cell, while the hydrophobic tails face each other in the middle of the membrane.

questions.question: Which non-metal is commonly used to preserve food materials?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen is a non-metal commonly used to preserve packaged food. It is an inert gas that displaces oxygen in food packaging, which prevents oxidation and spoilage. This method is called nitrogen flushing and is widely used in chips, snacks, and dry foods.

questions.question: Recently, which country became the first country to include the right to abortion in its constitution?
questions.explanation: France has made history by becoming the first nation to embed the right to abortion in its constitution. In a landmark joint parliamentary session, a resounding majority of 780 votes supported the constitutional amendment, while only 72 opposed it.

questions.question: In Bihar, which of the following dances is performed to please Indra, the rain god, for good rainfall and good harvest?
questions.explanation: Jhijhiya is the main folk dance of Mithila region of Bihar which is organized on the occasion of Durga Puja. This dance is performed to please Indra the god of rain, for good rainfall and good harvest in Bihar.

questions.question: Which of the following dancers is associated with odissi dance?
questions.explanation: Sujata Mohapatra is an Indian classical dancer and teacher of 'Odissi' dance style. His guru is 'Kelucharan Mahapatra.' In 2011 he founded an institute- ''Guru Kirti Srijan' in his home town Balasore. She is honored by the sangeet Natak Akademi Award in 2017 for her contribution to Odissi dance.

questions.question: In which state is the Minimata Dam, built on the Hasdeo River, located?
questions.explanation: The Minimata Dam, built on the Hasdeo River, is located in the state of Chhattisgarh, India. Hasdeo River is one of the important tributaries of the Mahanadi River, which flows primarily through Chhattisgarh.The dam is named after Minimata Agam Dass Guru, a prominent social reformer and politician who worked for the upliftment of the underprivileged, especially in the Scheduled Castes community.

questions.question: The boundary line between India and Pakistan is example of ______ .
questions.explanation: The boundary line between India and Pakistan is considered a classic example of a superimposed boundary, as it was drawn by a colonial power [the British] without considering the existing cultural and ethnic divisions in the region during the partition of India, leading to significant population displacement and ongoing conflict.

questions.question: Which of the following is a part of Sutta Pitaka?
questions.explanation: The Vinay Pitaka consists of rules of conduct & discipline applicable to the monastic life of monks & nuns. Sutta Pitaka consists of the main teaching or Dhamma of Buddha. It is divided into 5 Nikayas:– Digha Nikaya, Majjhima Nikayam, Samyutta Nikaya, Anguttara Nikaya & Khuddaka Nikaya. The Abhidamma Pitaka is a Philosophical analysis and systematization of the teaching of Monks.

questions.question: Which institution secured the 45th rank in Engineering and Technology of Quacquarelli Symonds [QS] World University Rankings 2024?
questions.explanation: IIT Bombay climbed to 45th in Engineering and Technology in the 2024 QS World University Rankings, scoring 79.1/100. The QS ranks universities in 55 disciplines; results released by the UK-based agency highlight IIT Bombay’s strengths in various fields including Minerals and Mining [25th], Data Science and AI [30th], Civil and Structural Engineering [42nd], and others.

questions.question: Who invented the ballpoint pen?
questions.explanation: The Hungarian brothers, Laszlo and George Biro, made the first ball point pen in 1894. It followed the first workable fountain pen which was invented by L.E. Waterman in 1884.

questions.question: Which article of the Constitution contains the provision of citizenship to person migrated to India from Pakistan?
questions.explanation: • Article 6 – Provides for the rights of citizenship of certain people who migrated to India from Pakistan. • Article 5 – Grants citizenship to people residing in India at the commencement of the Constitution. • Article 7 – Denies citizenship to those who migrated from India to Pakistan, unless they return with a resettlement permit. • Article 8 – Grants citizenship to Indians living abroad.

questions.question: Which of the following is a step that the central bank will take to increase the overall availability of credit?
options: It will sell the government securities in the market
It will buy more government securities from the market
It will raise the reverse repo rate
It will raise the repo rate
questions.explanation: To increase the availability of credit, a central bank can buy government securities, reduce the reserve requirement, or decrease the discount rate. These actions increase the money supply, which gives banks more funds to lend.

questions.question: Who was known as the Second founder of the Maratha Empire?
questions.explanation: Balaji Bishwanath was known as the second founder of the Maratha Empire. Balaji Bishwanath assisted the Maratha Emperor Shahu to consolidate his grip damaged by the attack of Mughal Rulers.

questions.question: Which of the following is the simplest member of organic compounds?
questions.explanation: Methane is the simplest member of the alkane family and indeed the simplest of organic compounds, as all other compounds are derived by altering this compound.

questions.question: Which is the system of unit?
questions.explanation: FPS System stands for Foot-Pound-Second system. It is one of the systems of units used to measure physical quantities like length [foot], mass [pound], and time [second].

questions.question: What is the thickness of the crust [in km] on the ocean floors?
questions.explanation: Crust is the outermost solid part of the earth. It is brittle in nature. The thickness of the crust varies under the oceanic and continental areas. Oceanic crust is thinner as compared to the continental crust. The thickness of oceanic crust is 5-10 km.

questions.question: In which country is the UNESCO World Heritage Site, Vjetrenica Cave located?
questions.explanation: Vjetrenica Cave, positioned in Ravno, Bosnia and Herzegovina, boasts remarkable biodiversity. As the world’s most biodiverse grotto, it houses over 200 animal species, with 90 troglobionts, creatures that live specifically in caves.

questions.question: Who was the revolutionary leader who ended his days as a Swami of Ramakrishna Mission?
questions.explanation: Jatindranath Bandopadhyay who is also known by the nickname "Niralamba Swami'. Jantindranath ended his days as a Swami of Ramkrishna Mission. He stayed with Maharishi Aurobindo in his revolutionary life. He was born on 19 November, 1877 and died in 1930.

questions.question: Recently, who has been elected as the new President of the Paralympic Committee of India [PCI]?
questions.explanation: Devendra Jhajharia, a two-time Paralympic gold medalist in javelin throw, is the newly elected President of the Paralympic Committee of India [PCI], succeeding Deepa Malik.

questions.question: Who described the preamble of Indian Constitution as the ' Political horoscope of the Indian Constitution'?
questions.explanation: Kanhaiyalal Maniklal Munshi described the preamble of Indian Constitution as the ' Political horoscope of the Indian Constitution' because it defined the establishment of governance in the country.

questions.question: PM-SVANidhi scheme is launched by which ministry?
options: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare>कृषि एवं किसान कल्याण मंत्रालय
Ministry of Consumer Affairs>उपभोक्ता मामले मंत्रालय
Ministry of Food Processing Industries >खाद्य प्रसंस्करण उद्योग मंत्रालय
Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs>आवास और शहरी मामलों का मंत्रालय
questions.explanation: The Commissioner of Jammu Municipal Corporation recently distributed Dogri-language PM SVANidhi plates to street vendors in Jammu and Kashmir. PM SVANidhi, launched by the Union Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs under Atma Nirbhar Bharat, provides micro-credit loans to street vendors. > जम्मू नगर निगम के आयुक्त ने हाल ही में जम्मू और कश्मीर में सड़क विक्रेताओं को डोगरी भाषा में पीएम स्वनिधि प्लेटें वितरित कीं। केंद्रीय आवास और शहरी मामलों के मंत्रालय द्वारा आत्मनिर्भर भारत के तहत शुरू की गई पीएम स्वनिधि, सड़क विक्रेताओं को माइक्रो-क्रेडिट ऋण प्रदान करती है।

questions.question: Which of the following is a common condition that develops when a sticky substance called plaque builds up inside your arteries, making them hard and narrow?
questions.explanation: Atherosclerosis is a disease that affects the blood vessels that carry oxygen-rich blood to the heart and other organs. Plaque is made of fat, cholesterol, calcium, and other substances that stick to the inner walls of the arteries. Over time, plaque can harden and narrow the arteries, reducing the blood flow and increasing the risk of blood clots. > एथेरोस्क्लेरोसिस एक ऐसी बीमारी है जो हृदय और अन्य अंगों तक ऑक्सीजन युक्त रक्त ले जाने वाली रक्त वाहिकाओं को प्रभावित करती है। प्लाक वसा, कोलेस्ट्रॉल, कैल्शियम और अन्य पदार्थों से बना होता है जो धमनियों की भीतरी दीवारों से चिपक जाते हैं। समय के साथ, प्लाक धमनियों को सख्त और संकीर्ण कर सकता है, जिससे रक्त प्रवाह कम हो जाता है और रक्त के थक्के बनने का खतरा बढ़ जाता है।

questions.explanation: By default price of water is Rs 0 per litre W M 0 40 35 5 35 1 7 =1:7

questions.question: A thief steals a van at 3:00 a.m. and drives it at a speed of 54 km/h. The thief is discovered at 4:00 a.m. and the owner starts the chase with another van at a speed of 72 km/h. At what time will he catch the thief?
questions.explanation: Distance covered by thief in one hour= 54 km/h ×1 = 54 km Relative Speed of thief and owner = 72 km/h-54 km/h=18 km/h Time = Distance/speed = 54/18= 3hr Time at which the thief will be caught= 4am+3hr = 7am

questions.question: The present age of Rahim is four times the present age of his daughter, Savita. Seven years from now, Rahim will be three times as old as Savita. What is the present age [in years] of Savita?
questions.explanation: Present age: R:S=4:1 Seven years from now: R:S=3:1 = t= 14years Savita’s age = =14years

questions.explanation: Equate the work: A×27 hours=-B×45 hours = Total Work = A×27 hours= 5×27 hours=135 Time = = =67.5hrs

questions.explanation: Actual Change Income 8 ; +2 [25%] Expenditure 5 ; +1.5[30%] Salary 3 ; +0.5 Percentage increase in his monthly savings= 100 = 50/3% increase

questions.question: If △XYZ≅△MNO, XY=2m+1, YZ=2p+2, XZ=7, MN=5, MO=2x-1 & NO=8. Find the value of m+x+p.
questions.explanation: △XYZ≅△MNO XY=MN, YZ=NO, XZ=MO XY=MN 2m+1=5 m=2 YZ=NO 2p+2=8 p=3 XZ=MO 7=2x-1 x=4 m+x+p= 2+3+4=9

questions.question: Urvashi sells a keyboard for ₹1,260 at a profit of 20%, and another keyboard for ₹1080 at a loss of 10%. What is her total gain or loss percentage?
questions.explanation: 20% profit = CP: SP =5:6→1260, CP=5×210=1050 10% Loss = CP: SP=10:9→1080, CP=10×120=1200 Total gain or loss=SP-CP=2340-2250=90 Total gain percentage =gain Cost Price ×100= =4% Gain

questions.question: If the semi-perimeter and area of a rectangular field whose length and breadth are ‘x’ and ‘y’ is 12 cm and 28 cm2, respectively, then find the value of x4 + x2y2 + y4.
questions.explanation: Semi perimeter of rectangle= length+ breadth= x+y=12, Area of rectangle= length × breadth = xy = 28 cm2

questions.question: The largest 5-digit number exactly divisible by 88 is.
questions.explanation: By divisibility rule of 8 & 11 99968 is exactly divisible by 88

questions.explanation: [a+b+c]2 – a2 - b2 - c2 = a2 + b2+ c2 +2[ab + bc + ca] – a2 - b2 - c2 =2[ab + bc + ca]

questions.question: The area of the sector of a circle of radius 18 cm is 36 π cm². Find the length of the corresponding arc of the sector [in cm].
questions.explanation: Area of the sector = ½×length of arc×radius 36 π = ½×length of arc×18 length of arc= 4 π

questions.question: To go a distance of 144 km upstream, a rower takes 18 hours while it takes her only 9 hours to row the same distance downstream. What is the speed of the Boat.
questions.explanation: Speed= Upstream =Speed of [B-S] = = 8km/h Downstream= Speed of [B+S] = = 16km/h Speed of the Boat= = =12 km/h

In a partnership Business, B’s capital was half of A’s. If after 8 months, B withdrew half of his capital and after 2 more months A withdrew 1/4th of his capital, then after one year the profit ratio of B and A will be:
questions.explanation: Let the Capital of A be 8x So, capital of B will be 4x A: B = [8x ×10+6x ×2] : [4x ×8+2x ×4] =92x:40x =23:10 B: A= 10:23

questions.question: A seller sold 17 cookies for ₹85 instead of 20 cookies in one kg, cheating a customer. What is the gain [in ₹] if the seller bought the cookies for ₹76?
questions.explanation: C.P of 1 cookie = 76/20 = 3.8 Profit = [S.P. – C.P] + C.P. of 3 cookies = 85 – 76 + 3× 3.8 = ₹20.4

questions.question: The Bar chart shows Petrol consumption [in Litres] for three persons A, B and C for months February to May. Give the answers of the following questions. In the given Bar chart, the percentage decrease in Petrol consumption of ‘B’ from April to May is:
questions.explanation: Percentage decrease =115-108115× 100 = 100 =6.08%

questions.question: The Bar chart shows Petrol consumption [in Litres] for three persons A, B and C for months February to May. Give the answers of the following questions. In the given Bar chart, maximum Petrol consumption has been carried out in which month:
The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
questions.explanation: The error lies in segment "He made me to". CONCEPT: • The verb "make" is a causative verb, and when it is used in the causative sense, it is followed by the base form [bare infinitive] of the verb, without "to." • Therefore, "He made me to apologize" is incorrect because the infinitive "to" should not be used here. • The correct sentence should use the bare infinitive "apologize" after "made." Here are some more examples for better understanding: • She made him go to the store. [Correct] • They made us wait for hours. [Correct] Correct Sentence: "He made me apologize for his mistake after the meeting."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. If ________ is favourable, we will go for a picnic.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Weather Explanation: The word "weather" is the most appropriate choice because it refers to the atmospheric conditions, such as temperature, precipitation, and wind, which can directly affect our decision to go for a walk. Here are the meanings and examples of all options: • Weather [Correct] – मौसम – Refers to the atmospheric conditions at a specific time [e.g., temperature, rain, wind]. Example: "If the weather permits, we will go for a walk." • Climate – जलवायु – Refers to the long-term patterns of weather in a region over a prolonged period. Example: "The climate in this region is very hot and dry." • Season – ऋतु – Refers to one of the four periods [spring, summer, autumn, winter] characterized by particular weather conditions. Example: "Summer is the best season to go to the beach." • Whether – क्या – Used to introduce alternatives or a choice between two possibilities. Example: "I don’t know whether he will come to the party." Therefore, "weather" is the best fit to express the idea of atmospheric conditions influencing the decision to go for a walk.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. A few word of gratitude can make a big difference in someone's day.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: A few words of Explanation: The sentence requires the use of "a few words of" to convey the correct meaning. "A few" indicates a small but sufficient quantity, and "words" should be in the plural form. The phrase "a few words of" is the proper way to express the idea that a small amount of something [in these cases, words] can have a meaningful impact. Corrected sentence: "A few words of gratitude can make a big difference in someone's day."

questions.question: Select the Grammatically correct sentence -
options: May she to achieve great success in her career and lead a fulfilling life.
Let she go to the party with her friends.
May the team wins the championship this year.
Let them enjoy the concert and have a great time.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: Let them enjoy the concert and have a great time. Explanation: • Option [D] "Let them enjoy the concert and have a great time." This is correct because after the word "let," we use the object pronoun "them," and the verbs "enjoy" and "have" are correctly in their base forms. This follows the rules for optative sentences where the verb after "let" should be in its base form. Why the other options are incorrect: • Option [A] "May she to achieve great success in her career and lead a fulfilling life." Error: The phrase "to achieve" is incorrect. After "may" in optative sentences, we use the base form of the verb without "to." Corrected Sentence: "May she achieve great success in her career and lead a fulfilling life." • Option [B] "Let she go to the party with her friends." Error: The pronoun "she" is used incorrectly. After "let," we should use the object pronoun "her," not the subject pronoun "she." Corrected Sentence: "Let her go to the party with her friends." • Option [C] "May the team wins the championship this year." Error: The verb "wins" is incorrect. After "may" in optative sentences, we use the base form of the verb. Corrected Sentence: "May the team win the championship this year."

questions.question: Identify the word that is correctly spelt.
questions.explanation: The correct spelling is "Exuberant." Exuberant [Correct] – उल्लासपूर्ण – To be filled with excitement, energy, or enthusiasm. Example: "The children were exuberant after receiving their prizes."

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. They had a big fight, but they made out by the end of the day.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Made up Explanation: In this sentence, the phrase "made up" is the most appropriate. "Make up" means to reconcile or resolve a disagreement after a quarrel, which fits with the idea of the individuals resolving their conflict by the end of the day. Here are the meanings and examples of all options: • Made out – समझना/सफल होना–achieving success in a challenging situation. Example: “He made out fine during the difficult project and earned the promotion.” • Made over – पुनर्निर्मित करना – To renovate, transform, or change the appearance of something. Example: "She made over the old house into a modern apartment." • Made up [Correct] – समझौता करना – To reconcile or resolve a disagreement, usually after a fight or argument. Example: "They had a big fight, but they made up by the end of the day." • Made in – निर्मित – Refers to the origin or place where something was produced. Example: "This chair was made in Italy." Corrected sentence: "They had a big fight, but they made up by the end of the day."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence - For a fleeting moment the Master reappeared.
questions.explanation: Sol: Correct Answer: Momentary Explanation: The word "fleeting" refers to something that lasts for a very short period of time. The most appropriate synonym for "fleeting" in this context is "momentary", which also means lasting for only a brief moment. Let's see the meanings of all the words: • Fleeting – क्षणिक – Lasting for a very short time. Example: "For a fleeting moment, he thought he had seen her again." • Momentary – क्षणिक – Lasting for a very short time, similar to fleeting. Example: "The pain was momentary, and soon it passed." • Magical – जादुई – Something that is enchanting or extraordinary. Example: "The night felt magical as the stars lit up the sky." • Steady – स्थिर – Constant or unchanging over time. Example: "He gave a steady performance throughout the competition." • Critical – महत्वपूर्ण – Extremely important or crucial, often in a difficult or decisive situation. Example: "She made a critical decision that changed the outcome of the game." Corrected Sentence: "For a momentary moment, the Master reappeared."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given word. MALLEABLE
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: Intractable Explanation: The word "malleable" refers to something that is easily shaped, influenced, or adaptable. The opposite of malleable, which suggests flexibility and ease of manipulation, is "intractable," meaning something that is difficult or impossible to control or influence. Let’s see the meanings of all the options along with their usage: • Malleable – लचीला / बदलने योग्य – Refers to something that can be shaped or altered easily, especially when discussing metals or personalities. Example: "Gold is malleable and can be hammered into thin sheets." • Intractable – जिद्दी / कठोर – Refers to something that is difficult to manage, control, or influence. This is the direct opposite of malleable. Example: "The problem was intractable and couldn’t be solved with the current methods." • Flexible – लचीला – Refers to something that can bend or adapt Example: "The material is flexible and can be stretched without breaking." • Fragile – नाजुक – Refers to something that is delicate or easily broken. It’s not the opposite of malleable, as malleable objects can be bent or shaped without breaking. Example: "The fragile vase shattered when it hit the floor." • Amenable – सहायक – Refers to someone who is open to suggestions or influence, making it similar in meaning to malleable Example: "She was amenable to the idea of working together on the project."

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. That week the dollar dropped _______ its lowest levels.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: To Explanation: The correct preposition to use with "dropped" in this context is "to." When referring to a movement or change in level or position, "to" indicates the direction toward a specific point or limit. In this case, the dollar dropped to its lowest levels, implying it reached that specific point. Corrected Sentence: "That week the dollar dropped to its lowest levels."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning for the given idiom. Butterflies in the stomach
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: To be anxious and nervous. Explanation: The idiom "butterflies in the stomach" refers to the feeling of nervousness or anxiety that people often experience before an important event, performance, or situation. This sensation is often described as a fluttery or uneasy feeling in the stomach, similar to how butterflies might flutter around. Origin of the Idiom: The idiom originates from the physical sensation of nervousness, which can feel like small movements or flutters in the stomach. This "butterfly" feeling is often associated with anxiety or anticipation before something significant. Example: "Before giving her speech, she had butterflies in her stomach." Hindi – "अपनी भाषण देने से पहले, उसे पेट में तितलियाँ सी महसूस हो रही थीं।" Therefore, the correct meaning of the idiom "butterflies in the stomach" is "to be anxious and nervous."

questions.question: Identify the segment in the sentence that contains grammatical error.
questions.explanation: Error in segment: have remained a classic Explanation: • The title "The Three Musketeers" refers to a single book [singular in meaning], even though the words "three" and "musketeers" are plural. This is why a singular verb ["has remained"] should be used instead of the plural verb "have remained." Here are some more examples for better understanding: • "The Lord of the Rings has been adapted into several movies." • The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes has been a popular detective novel. Corrected Sentence: “The Three Musketeers has remained a classic novel for many years."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate SYNONYM of the given word. RECOLLECT
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Remember. Explanation: The word "recollect" means to recall or remember something from the past. The synonym that closely matches this meaning is "remember," which refers to the act of bringing something back to mind or recalling a past experience or thought. Let’s see the meaning of all the words: • Recollect – याद करना – To recall or remember something from the past. Example: "I tried to recollect the details of the meeting." • Dodge – बचना – To avoid or escape something. Example: "He dodged the question with a quick answer." • Save – बचाना – To keep something for later or protect it. Example: "She saved her work on the computer." • Repeat – दोहराना – To say or do something again, which is not a synonym for recollect. Example: "Can you repeat what you just said?" • Remember – याद रखना – To bring something to mind, the closest match to "recollect." Example: "She tried to remember what she had learned in class."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the underlined word in the following sentence- They didn't even have the COURTESY to apologize.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Rudeness. Explanation: The word "courtesy" refers to polite behaviour, kindness, or respect toward others. The opposite of courtesy would be "rudeness," which denotes a lack of manners, respect, or politeness. Let’s see the meaning of all the words: • Courtesy – शिष्टता – Politeness, kindness, or respectful behavior toward others. Example: "She showed great courtesy when meeting new people." • Rudeness – अशिष्टता – The lack of respect, politeness, or good manners. Example: "His rudeness towards the guests was very noticeable." • Boldness – साहस – Courage or willingness to take risks, unrelated to politeness or manners. Example: "His boldness allowed him to stand up and speak his mind." • Courage – साहस – The ability to face fear or danger, unrelated to politeness or respect. Example: "She showed great courage during the difficult situation." • Insecurity – असुरक्षा – A lack of confidence, unrelated to politeness or manners. Example: "His insecurity made him hesitate to express his opinion."

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blanks. The _______ of the perfume costs more than a _______.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Scent, Cent. Explanation of all Homonyms: • Scent – खुशबू – Refers to a fragrance or smell, particularly a pleasant one like that of a perfume. Example: "The scent of the perfume lingered in the air." • Cent – सेंट – A unit of currency, often used to refer to coins, particularly in small denominations. Example: "The candy only costs a few cents." • Sent – भेजा – The past tense of "send," meaning to have dispatched or delivered something. Example: "She sent the letter to her friend." Correct Answer Explanation: The sentence should read: "The scent of the perfume costs more than a cent." This is the correct choice because "scent" refers to the fragrance of the perfume and "cent" refers to a small unit of currency, making the sentence logically sound.

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words. Fit to be eaten
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Edible. Explanation: • Audible – श्रव्य – Refers to something that can be heard. Example: "The sound was barely audible due to the noise in the room." • Glutton – लालची व्यक्ति – A person who eats to excess or is greedy about food. Example: "The glutton devoured the entire pizza by himself." • Healthy – स्वस्थ – Refers to something that is beneficial to health, but not necessarily fit to be eaten. Example: "Eating vegetables is healthy for your body." • Edible – खाने योग्य – Refers to something that is fit to be eaten or safe to consume. Example: "These berries are edible and can be added to your salad." Therefore, "edible" is the correct choice, as it describes something that is suitable for eating.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. The changes benefited management, but left the workers out in the cold.
options: Sent in cold or chilly places to visit
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Excluded or ignored. Explanation: The idiom "left out in the cold" refers to being excluded or ignored from something, often a group or situation. It implies that someone is left out or neglected, while others are included or benefited. Origin of the Idiom: The phrase "left out in the cold" originates from the idea of being physically outside in the cold, symbolizing isolation or neglect. It conveys the feeling of being disregarded or not part of the group that is receiving benefits or attention. Example: "Although the new policy helped the executives, it left the workers out in the cold, with no improvements for them." Therefore, the correct meaning of the idiom "left out in the cold" is "excluded or ignored."

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words given. One who has suddenly gained new wealth, power or prestige
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Parvenu. Explanation of all Options: • Tycoon – धनी व्यापारी – A wealthy and influential person in business or industry. Example: "He is a tycoon in the tech industry." • Parvenu – नवधनाढ्य व्यक्ति – A person who has suddenly gained wealth, power, or prestige, often in a way that is seen as lacking the refinement of traditional wealth. Example: "The parvenu was rapidly gaining influence in the city, though some still looked down on him." • Billionaire – अरबपति – A person with a net worth of at least a billion units of currency, often not related to sudden acquisition. Example: "The billionaire made his fortune in real estate." • Entrepreneur – उद्यमी – A person who starts and runs a business, taking on financial risks. Example: "The entrepreneur launched a successful tech start up." Therefore, "parvenu" is the correct word for who has suddenly gained new wealth, power or prestige.

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph. A. Sitting in the Victor Force operations room in Awantipura. B. And inquired when we had last patrolled that area. C. He suddenly pointed a laser light to a part of the heights above Tral. D. The General asked a very important question. > Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph. A. Sitting in the Victor Force operations room in Awantipura. B. And inquired when we had last patrolled that area. C. He suddenly pointed a laser light to a part of the heights above Tral. D. The General asked a very important question.
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: ADBC Explanation: • A sets the scene: "Sitting in the Victor Force operations room in Awantipura." • D introduces the key event: "The General asked a very important question." • B follows with what the question was about: "And inquired when we had last patrolled that area." • C concludes with the General’s action: "He suddenly pointed a laser light to a part of the heights above Tral." Final Paragraph: Sitting in the Victor Force operations room in Awantipura, The General asked a very important question. And inquired when we had last patrolled that area. He suddenly pointed a laser light to a part of the heights above Tral. >Correct Answer: ADBC Explanation: • A sets the scene: "Sitting in the Victor Force operations room in Awantipura." • D introduces the key event: "The General asked a very important question." • B follows with what the question was about: "And inquired when we had last patrolled that area." • C concludes with the General’s action: "He suddenly pointed a laser light to a part of the heights above Tral." Final Paragraph: Sitting in the Victor Force operations room in Awantipura, The General asked a very important question. And inquired when we had last patrolled that area. He suddenly pointed a laser light to a part of the heights above Tral.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to change the voice of the given sentence. None likes him.
options: He was not liked by anyone.
He has not been liked by anyone.
He is not liked by anyone.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: He is not liked by anyone. Explanation: The given sentence in active voice is: None likes him. This follows the structure: Subject + Verb + Object → None [subject] + likes [verb] + him [object]. To convert this into passive voice, the object "him" becomes the subject and the verb "likes" changes to its passive form "is liked." The sentence then follows the structure: Object + am/are/is + past participle + by + subject. So, the passive voice form of the sentence is: He is not liked by anyone. • The object "him" becomes the subject "he." • The verb "likes" is changed to "is liked." • "None" is replaced with "anyone" to convey a more natural passive structure.

questions.question: Change the narration of the given sentence. He said, "I was writing a letter yesterday."
options: He said that he will be writing a letter the next day.
He said that he had been writing a letter the day before.
He said that he had written a letter the day before.
He said that he would be writing a letter the next day.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: He said that he had been writing a letter the day before. Explanation: • The given sentence in direct speech is: "I was writing a letter yesterday," said he. • To convert this into indirect speech, we follow the following rules: • When converting this into indirect speech, the reporting verb "said" remains the same • The past continuous tense "was writing" in direct speech is converted to the past perfect continuous tense "had been writing" in indirect speech because the sentence refers to an action that was happening in the past, prior to the time of reporting. • The word "yesterday" in direct speech is converted to "the day before" in indirect speech to maintain the proper time shift.

questions.question: A popular Russian saying suggests that when purchasing a home, one should focus more on the neighbourhood than the house itself. In the past, it was challenging to control who your neighbours ____1____ be. However, with the advent of the Internet and the group buying model, it’s now possible to create your dream community when buying a home. The concept of group buying, once ____2____ in industries like automobiles, baby products, and pet care, is now gaining traction in the real estate sector. Numerous websites and brokerage firms in India are offering group deals on real estate projects, making this trend more ____3____ to homebuyers. The process is straightforward. For instance, take the example of xxx.com, an integrated online and offline platform that showcases properties. This platform uses social media networks to inform potential buyers about attractive deals. Buyers can then ____4____ in viral marketing to gather a large group of interested buyers, enabling them to negotiate a better discount with the developer. The group buying model ____5____ heavily on customer participation. However, some wonder if this model is just a passing trend. It bears similarities to developers working with speculators who purchase properties in bulk before a project begins, often receiving discounts of 30% to 40%. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 1 -
questions.explanation: Explanation – The sentence "In the past, it was challenging to control who your neighbours would be" implies that it was difficult to predict or determine who your neighbours would turn out to be. The verb "would" is used to express a future-in-the-past or a hypothetical situation, which fits perfectly in this context.

questions.question: A popular Russian saying suggests that when purchasing a home, one should focus more on the neighbourhood than the house itself. In the past, it was challenging to control who your neighbours ____1____ be. However, with the advent of the Internet and the group buying model, it’s now possible to create your dream community when buying a home. The concept of group buying, once ____2____ in industries like automobiles, baby products, and pet care, is now gaining traction in the real estate sector. Numerous websites and brokerage firms in India are offering group deals on real estate projects, making this trend more ____3____ to homebuyers. The process is straightforward. For instance, take the example of xxx.com, an integrated online and offline platform that showcases properties. This platform uses social media networks to inform potential buyers about attractive deals. Buyers can then ____4____ in viral marketing to gather a large group of interested buyers, enabling them to negotiate a better discount with the developer. The group buying model ____5____ heavily on customer participation. However, some wonder if this model is just a passing trend. It bears similarities to developers working with speculators who purchase properties in bulk before a project begins, often receiving discounts of 30% to 40%. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 2 -
questions.explanation: Explanation - The correct answer is Option C: applied. The sentence implies that the concept of group buying was already being used or applied in other industries before it gained traction in the real estate sector. Other options like "denied", "rejected", and "acquitted" don't fit the context as they convey opposite or unrelated meanings.

questions.question: A popular Russian saying suggests that when purchasing a home, one should focus more on the neighbourhood than the house itself. In the past, it was challenging to control who your neighbours ____1____ be. However, with the advent of the Internet and the group buying model, it’s now possible to create your dream community when buying a home. The concept of group buying, once ____2____ in industries like automobiles, baby products, and pet care, is now gaining traction in the real estate sector. Numerous websites and brokerage firms in India are offering group deals on real estate projects, making this trend more ____3____ to homebuyers. The process is straightforward. For instance, take the example of xxx.com, an integrated online and offline platform that showcases properties. This platform uses social media networks to inform potential buyers about attractive deals. Buyers can then ____4____ in viral marketing to gather a large group of interested buyers, enabling them to negotiate a better discount with the developer. The group buying model ____5____ heavily on customer participation. However, some wonder if this model is just a passing trend. It bears similarities to developers working with speculators who purchase properties in bulk before a project begins, often receiving discounts of 30% to 40%. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 3 -
questions.explanation: Explanation – The correct answer is Option D: accessible. The sentence implies that the trend of group buying in real estate is becoming more available or reachable to homebuyers due to numerous websites and brokerage firms offering group deals. "Accessible" fits perfectly in this context, conveying the idea that homebuyers can now easily avail of this option. Other options don't fit as well: - "Desirable" implies attractiveness or appeal, which isn't the primary focus here. - "Hummable" is unrelated to the context (it refers to a catchy tune). - "Culpable" implies blame or responsibility, which doesn't fit the context.

questions.question: A popular Russian saying suggests that when purchasing a home, one should focus more on the neighbourhood than the house itself. In the past, it was challenging to control who your neighbours ____1____ be. However, with the advent of the Internet and the group buying model, it’s now possible to create your dream community when buying a home. The concept of group buying, once ____2____ in industries like automobiles, baby products, and pet care, is now gaining traction in the real estate sector. Numerous websites and brokerage firms in India are offering group deals on real estate projects, making this trend more ____3____ to homebuyers. The process is straightforward. For instance, take the example of xxx.com, an integrated online and offline platform that showcases properties. This platform uses social media networks to inform potential buyers about attractive deals. Buyers can then ____4____ in viral marketing to gather a large group of interested buyers, enabling them to negotiate a better discount with the developer. The group buying model ____5____ heavily on customer participation. However, some wonder if this model is just a passing trend. It bears similarities to developers working with speculators who purchase properties in bulk before a project begins, often receiving discounts of 30% to 40%. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 4 -
questions.explanation: Explanation - The correct answer is Option A: engage. The sentence implies that buyers can participate or take part in viral marketing to gather a large group of interested buyers. "Engage in" is a common idiomatic expression that means to participate or take part in an activity. In this context, "engage in viral marketing" fits perfectly, conveying the idea of actively participating in marketing efforts to spread the word. Other options don't fit as well: - "Gauge" implies measuring or assessing something. - "Encourage" implies promoting or supporting something, but it doesn't fit grammatically in this context. - "Prestige" is a noun that refers to reputation or status, and it doesn't fit grammatically or semantically in this context.

questions.question: A popular Russian saying suggests that when purchasing a home, one should focus more on the neighbourhood than the house itself. In the past, it was challenging to control who your neighbours ____1____ be. However, with the advent of the Internet and the group buying model, it’s now possible to create your dream community when buying a home. The concept of group buying, once ____2____ in industries like automobiles, baby products, and pet care, is now gaining traction in the real estate sector. Numerous websites and brokerage firms in India are offering group deals on real estate projects, making this trend more ____3____ to homebuyers. The process is straightforward. For instance, take the example of xxx.com, an integrated online and offline platform that showcases properties. This platform uses social media networks to inform potential buyers about attractive deals. Buyers can then ____4____ in viral marketing to gather a large group of interested buyers, enabling them to negotiate a better discount with the developer. The group buying model ____5____ heavily on customer participation. However, some wonder if this model is just a passing trend. It bears similarities to developers working with speculators who purchase properties in bulk before a project begins, often receiving discounts of 30% to 40%. Choose the most appropriate option to fill in the blank 5 -
questions.explanation: Explanation - The correct answer is Option C: relies. The sentence implies that the group buying model depends heavily on customer participation. "Relies on" is a common idiomatic expression that means to depend or be dependent on something or someone. In this context, "relies heavily on customer participation" fits perfectly, conveying the idea that the model's success is heavily dependent on customers actively participating. Other options don't fit as well: - "Advocates" implies supporting or promoting something. - "Desires" implies wanting or wishing for something, which doesn't fit the context. - "Supplies" implies providing or furnishing something, which doesn't fit the context.
